sections.paragraph: m. (from the root קָלָה No.I.), and קָלִיא (with א otiose, like נָקִי, נָקִיא), 1Sa 17:17, m. something roasted, parched, i.e. grains of wheat, or barley roasted in the ears (see Macmichael’s Journey, p. 235), such as the Arabs, both ancient and modern, eat, Lev 23:14 1Sa 25:18 2Sa 17:28 Rth 2:14.
